action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|...#.A...|
 2|...B.#...|
 3|#..###...|
 4|.##..#.p#|
 5|........2|
 6|.....x...|
 7|.S.y#.#..|
 8|q......1#|
 9|.##...#.#|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (2,7) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (2,7), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (6,1): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {0}. So it is open at t=0,2,... and closed at t=1,3,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (4,2): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {1, 2}. So it is open at t=1,2,5,6,... and closed at t=0,3,4,7,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (8,4); t mod 8=1: (8,5); t mod 8=2: (8,6); t mod 8=3: (8,7); t mod 8=4: (9,7); t mod 8=5: (8,7); t mod 8=6: (8,6); t mod 8=7: (8,5). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (1,8); t mod 4=1: (2,8); t mod 4=2: (3,8); t mod 4=3: (2,8). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (8,8) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (6,6) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (9,5) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (4,7) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 35 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 7 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I need to deliver two parcels. Parcel 1: pick up at (8,8), deliver to (6,6). Parcel 2: pick up at (9,5), deliver to (4,7). I start at (2,7) at t=0.

First, I should plan a route to pick up both parcels and deliver them. Let me think about the most efficient path.

From (2,7), I need to get to (8,8) to pick up parcel 1, then to (6,6) to deliver it. Then to (9,5) to pick up parcel 2, then to (4,7) to deliver it.

Looking at the map:
- (8,8) is in the bottom right area
- (6,6) is near the center
- (9,5) is on the right edge
- (4,7) is in the middle-left area

I should start by heading towards (8,8). From (2,7), I can go E to get closer to the right side.

Let me plan my first 5 moves: E E E E E to get from (2,7) to (7,7). This gets me closer to (8,8).

POSITION: (7,7)
PLAN: E E E E E
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 1 RESULT (ticks 0 -> 5):
t=1: E -> OK, now at (3,7).
t=2: E -> OK, now at (4,7).
t=3: E -> BUMP (you ran into a wall), still at (4,7).
t=4: E -> BUMP (you ran into a wall), still at (4,7).
t=5: E -> BUMP (you ran into a wall), still at (4,7).
STATUS: tick 5 of 35. You are at (4,7). Carrying: nothing. Delivered: nothing. Rounds left: 6.
Respond now in the required format: a POSITION line predicting your cell after your next plan, then a PLAN line with exactly 5 moves.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I'm at (4,7) after 5 ticks. I need to pick up parcel 1 at (8,8) and parcel 2 at (9,5), then deliver them to (6,6) and (4,7) respectively.

Looking at the map, (4,7) is where parcel 2 needs to be delivered (glyph 'y'). But I haven't picked it up yet - it's at (9,5). I should go to (9,5) first to pick up parcel 2, then to (8,8) to pick up parcel 1, then deliver parcel 1 to (6,6), and finally deliver parcel 2 to (4,7).

From (4,7), I need to go to (9,5). Looking at the map:
- (4,7) is row 7, column 4
- (9,5) is row 5, column 9

I should go S to row 8, then E to column 9, then N to row 5. Or I could go E first then N.

Let me go: S S E E E to get from (4,7) to (4,9), then I need to go E to (9,9) then N to (9,5). Actually, let me reconsider.

From (4,7): S to (4,8), then E E E E to (8,8), then N N to (6,8), then E to (7,8), then N to (6,7), then N to (6,6) - but that's for parcel 1 delivery.

Actually, let me go to (9,5) first. From (4,7): S to (4,8), E E E E to (8,8), then N to (8,7), E to (9,7), N to (9,6), N to (9,5). That's S E E E E N N N - 8 moves.

For 5 moves, I'll go: S E E E E to get to (8,7).

POSITION: (8,7)
PLAN: S E E E E
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 2 RESULT (ticks 5 -> 10):
t=6: S -> OK, now at (4,8).
t=7: E -> OK, now at (5,8).
t=8: E -> OK, now at (6,8).
t=9: E -> OK, now at (7,8).
t=10: E -> OK, now at (8,8). PICKED UP parcel 1.
STATUS: tick 10 of 35. You are at (8,8). Carrying: parcel 1. Delivered: nothing. Rounds left: 5.
Respond now in the required format: a POSITION line predicting your cell after your next plan, then a PLAN line with exactly 5 moves.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I'm at (8,8) and have picked up parcel 1. Now I need to deliver it to (6,6). From (8,8), I need to go to (6,6). Looking at the map, (6,6) is row 6, column 6. I should go N N to (6,8), then W W to (6,6). That's 4 moves. I have 5 moves in this plan, so I'll add one more move - maybe go to (6,7) first or just wait.

Actually, let me go: N N W W N to get to (6,7), then I'll need to go N to (6,6) in the next plan. Or N N W W W to get to (5,8), then N N to (5,6), then E to (6,6).

Let me go: N N W W N to get to (6,7).

POSITION: (6,7)
PLAN: N N W W N
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 3 RESULT (ticks 10 -> 15):
t=11: N -> CAUGHT by guard P at (8,7)! Returned to start (2,7).
Capture penalty: the remaining 4 move(s) of your plan were cancelled and the round's leftover ticks were forfeited; the clock jumped to t=15.
STATUS: tick 15 of 35. You are at (2,7). Carrying: parcel 1. Delivered: nothing. Rounds left: 4.
Respond now in the required format: a POSITION line predicting your cell after your next plan, then a PLAN line with exactly 5 moves.

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I was caught by guard P at (8,7) at t=11. I'm back at (2,7) at t=15, still carrying parcel 1. I need to deliver parcel 1 to (6,6) and parcel 2 to (4,7). Parcel 2 is at (9,5), so I need to pick that up too.

Let me think about guard positions at t=15:
- Guard P: t mod 8 = 15 mod 8 = 7, so P is at (8,5)
- Guard Q: t mod 4 = 15 mod 4 = 3, so Q is at (2,8)

I should avoid (8,5) and (2,8). From (2,7), I can go E to avoid Q at (2,8). Let me go to (9,5) to pick up parcel 2 first, then to (6,6) to deliver parcel 1, then to (4,7) to deliver parcel 2.

From (2,7) to (9,5): E E E E E to (7,7), then S S to (7,9), then E E to (9,9), then N N to (9,7), then N to (9,6), then N to (9,5). That's too many moves.

Let me go: E E E E S to (7,8), then S to (7,9), then E E to (9,9), then N N N to (9,6). Hmm, still not (9,5).

Actually from (2,7): E E E E S S E E N N N to (9,5). That's 11 moves. For 5 moves, I'll go: E E E E S to (7,8).

POSITION: (7,8)
PLAN: E E E E S

I should avoid (9,7) and (1,8). From (4,8), I can go E to get towards (9,5). Let me go: E E E E S to (8,9), then I need to go N N to (8,7), but that's where P was at t=15. At t=21, P will be at (8,4). So (8,7) should be safe.

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I'm at (9,5) at t=30, carrying both parcels. I need to deliver parcel 1 to (6,6) and parcel 2 to (4,7). I have 5 moves left (t=30-34), and the game ends when I deliver the last parcel.

Guard positions at t=30:
- Guard P: t mod 8 = 30 mod 8 = 6, so P is at (8,6)
- Guard Q: t mod 4 = 30 mod 4 = 2, so Q is at (3,8)

I should avoid (8,6) and (3,8). From (9,5), I need to go to (6,6) first to deliver parcel 1. From (9,5), I can go W W W to (6,5), then N to (6,6). That's 4 moves. Then I need to go to (4,7) to deliver parcel 2. From (6,6), I can go W W to (4,6), then S to (4,7). That's 3 more moves.

But I only have 5 moves. Let me think again. I need to deliver both parcels within 5 moves. 

From (9,5): W W W N to (6,6) - delivers parcel 1. Then I need to go to (4,7). From (6,6): W W S to (4,7) - delivers parcel 2. That's 6 moves total.

I have 5 moves. Let me try: W W W N W to (5,6), then in the next plan I can go W S to (4,7). But I need to deliver parcel 1 at (6,6) first.

Actually, let me go: W W W N to (6,6) - this delivers parcel 1. Then I need to deliver parcel 2 at (4,7). From (6,6), I can go W W S to (4,7). That's 3 more moves. Total 4 moves to deliver both. I have 5 moves, so I can add one more move or wait.

Let me go: W W W N W S to (4,7). Wait, that's 6 moves. Let me do: W W W N W to (5,6), then the next plan will deliver parcel 2.

Actually, I should deliver parcel 1 first at (6,6), then parcel 2 at (4,7). Let me go: W W W N to (6,6) - delivers parcel 1. Then I need to go to (4,7). From (6,6), W W S is 3 moves to (4,7). Total 4 moves. I have 5 moves, so I can add one more move at the end.

Let me go: W W W N W S to (4,7). No, that's 6 moves. Let me do: W W W N W to (5,6), then in the next plan W S to (4,7).

Actually, let me just go: W W W N W S and see if it works. W W W N is 4 moves to (6,6), then W S is 2 more moves to (4,7). That's 6 moves. I need to split this.

First plan: W W W N to (6,6) - delivers parcel 1.
Second plan: W W S to (4,7) - delivers parcel 2.
